label: Adversarial robustness & security testing

node.label: Adversarial robustness & security testing
node.definition: Adversarial security evaluation probes evasion, extraction, prompt injection, tool abuse, data poisoning, privacy leakage, and supply-chain compromise.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Adversarial robustness & security testing is to connect Adversarial examples & adversarial training, Robustness, distribution shift & OOD evaluation to downstream capabilities including Red teaming, abuse testing & threat modeling, Fault tolerance, security, energy & cost optimization, Cybersecurity, finance & operational risk AI.
examples: substrate for Red teaming, abuse testing & threat modeling
substrate for Fault tolerance, security, energy & cost optimization
substrate for Cybersecurity, finance & operational risk AI
limitations: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Adversarial examples & adversarial training, Robustness, distribution shift & OOD evaluation.
